root_text.sn56.56:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, mahāpathavī parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya ṭhapetvā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā.
root_text.sn56.56:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yaṁ vā mahāpathaviyā parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ, yā vā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā avasiṭṭhā”ti?
root_text.sn56.56:1.4: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ mahāpathaviyā yadidaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ; appamattikā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā avasiṭṭhā.
root_text.sn56.56:1.5: Saṅkhampi na upenti, upanidhampi na upenti, kalabhāgampi na upenti mahāpathaviyā parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ upanidhāya satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā avasiṭṭhā”ti.

translation_text.sn56.56:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose the great earth was worn away and eroded except for seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds.
translation_text.sn56.56:1.3: Which is more: the great earth that has been worn away and eroded, or the seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds that are left?”
translation_text.sn56.56:1.4: “Sir, the great earth that has been worn away and eroded is certainly more. The seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds are tiny.
translation_text.sn56.56:1.5: Compared to the great earth that has been worn away and eroded, they don’t count, there’s no comparison, they’re not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.56:1.6: “In the same way, for a noble disciple …
translation_text.sn56.56:1.7: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
